jokes.text: Who would want to spend a weekend at a beach house with these idiots?
jokes.setup: Radio hosts excitedly promoting their beach house contest
jokes.punchline: Dennis dismissing the very prize Mac is desperately trying to win
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Dennis's elitist disdain contrasted with Mac's desperation. Economical and well-integrated.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Who would want to spend a weekend at a beach house with these idiots?

jokes.text: Where's the 'H' key? There's no 'H' key!
jokes.setup: Mac needs to spell 'hockey' quickly for trivia contest
jokes.punchline: He can't find the H key on a standard keyboard
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Mac's panic-induced stupidity. The absurdity of not finding H on a keyboard is perfectly executed.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Where's the 'H' key? There's no 'H' key!

jokes.text: I gotta think like that guy that sits on the rock... The Thinker... Oh, the Hulk! The Incredible Hulk!
jokes.setup: Mac trying to remember who The Thinker is
jokes.punchline: Charlie confuses The Thinker sculpture with the Hulk
jokes.explanation: Good character-based confusion with Charlie's typical non-sequitur logic.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I gotta think like that guy that sits on the rock... The Thinker...

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, the Hulk! The Incredible Hulk!

jokes.text: Hulk doesn't think at all!
jokes.setup: Charlie suggests the Hulk as The Thinker
jokes.punchline: Mac correctly points out the Hulk's defining trait is not thinking
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective observation that shows Mac occasionally has moments of clarity.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Hulk doesn't think at all!

jokes.text: You guys can't censor me. I'm a bit of a badass.
jokes.setup: Radio hosts ask Mac to stop cursing
jokes.punchline: Mac's inflated self-image as a 'badass' over saying mild profanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac delusion - thinking cursing on radio makes him a badass. Classic character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You guys can't censor me. I'm a bit of a badass.

jokes.text: This is my chance to finally sow myself into the inner circle.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining his beach house opportunity
jokes.punchline: Says 'sow' instead of 'sew' - wrong agricultural metaphor
jokes.explanation: Subtle malapropism that shows Mac's limited vocabulary while trying to sound sophisticated.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: This is my chance to finally sow myself into the inner circle.

jokes.text: Frank, nobody gives a shit about minutes anymore.
jokes.setup: Frank complaining about cell phone minutes being used
jokes.punchline: Dennis dismissing an outdated concern with typical arrogance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's dismissive attitude toward Frank's old-fashioned concerns.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Frank, nobody gives a shit about minutes anymore.

jokes.text: Actually, you know what? I do give a shit about minutes. I know I shouldn't and I'm just probably buying into the phone company's propaganda. But there you go.
jokes.setup: Dennis just said nobody cares about minutes
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself and overthinks it
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dennis moment - his narcissistic need to be right leads to immediate self-contradiction and pseudo-intellectual analysis.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Actually, you know what? I do give a shit about minutes. I know I shouldn't and I'm just probably buying into the phone company's propaganda. But there you go.

jokes.text: Because they got great banter. They jib-jab. Jib-jab, jib-jab, jib-jab.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining why he tapes Mac and Charlie
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical description of conversation as 'jib-jab'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Frank moment. 'Jib-jab' is an invented word that somehow perfectly describes meaningless chatter.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Because they got great banter. They jib-jab. Jib-jab, jib-jab, jib-jab.

jokes.text: We have voices. We talk about the issues.
jokes.setup: Dennis comparing himself to radio hosts
jokes.punchline: His pretentious belief that he has important things to say
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis narcissism - believing his opinions are inherently valuable and important.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We have voices. We talk about the issues.

jokes.text: Oh! Zing! Boom-boom!
jokes.setup: Someone makes a mild comeback about Mac not being able to spell hockey
jokes.punchline: Mac's over-the-top celebration of a weak burn
jokes.explanation: Mac's enthusiasm for even mild mockery directed at him shows his desperate need for any kind of attention.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh! Zing! Boom-boom!

jokes.text: Do not speak until I turn my tape over... I taped over my Luther Vandross mix!
jokes.setup: Frank being very serious about recording the gang
jokes.punchline: His horror at ruining his Luther Vandross tape
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from Frank being controlling to revealing his unexpected Luther Vandross obsession.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Do not speak until I turn my tape over... I taped over my Luther Vandross mix!

jokes.text: We are not men who get a lot of opportunities, Charlie... We've squandered 'em all.
jokes.setup: Mac talking about their big opportunity
jokes.punchline: Their mutual acknowledgment of their complete failure at life
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest self-awareness from Mac and Charlie about their perpetual failure. Well-earned character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We are not men who get a lot of opportunities, Charlie... We've squandered 'em all.

jokes.text: All of my instincts and my training tell me to use this as a weapon.
jokes.setup: Charlie teaching Mac to hold a hockey stick
jokes.punchline: Mac's natural instinct is violence, even with sports equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac character moment - his delusional martial arts background makes him see everything as a weapon.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: All of my instincts and my training tell me to use this as a weapon.

jokes.text: You look like a G.I. Joe action figure. Loosen up.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to get into hockey position
jokes.punchline: Charlie's comparison to a stiff action figure
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup - we can picture Mac's ridiculous stiff pose.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You look like a G.I. Joe action figure. Loosen up.

jokes.text: Bro, when you tack on mass you sacrifice flexibility. That's just a straight-up fact!
jokes.setup: Charlie criticizing Mac's stiffness
jokes.punchline: Mac's pseudo-scientific justification for being out of shape
jokes.explanation: Iconic Mac line. His delusional belief in his fitness combined with fake scientific authority. Became a major quotable moment.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Bro, when you tack on mass you sacrifice flexibility. That's just a straight-up fact!

jokes.text: What am I, a gymnast?
jokes.setup: Charlie asking Mac to touch his toes
jokes.punchline: Mac's indignant response as if basic flexibility is unreasonable
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the mass/flexibility joke. Mac treats basic movement as an extreme athletic demand.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What am I, a gymnast?

jokes.text: like a tiny bowl of cream you don't want to spill
jokes.setup: Charlie teaching Mac how to guide the puck gently
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific and delicate metaphor for hockey
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Charlie metaphor. The image of carefully carrying cream is hilariously inappropriate for hockey.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: like a tiny bowl of cream you don't want to spill

jokes.text: Otherwise I go out there, I look like a jabroni.
jokes.setup: Mac rejecting Charlie's gentle approach
jokes.punchline: First use of 'jabroni' - Mac's new favorite word
jokes.explanation: Introduction of 'jabroni' which becomes a running gag. Mac's adoption of wrestling terminology for general use.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Otherwise I go out there, I look like a jabroni.

jokes.text: Gliding like a jabroni is what I'm gonna be doing.
jokes.setup: Charlie insisting Mac practice gliding first
jokes.punchline: Mac using 'jabroni' again, showing he doesn't fully understand it
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Mac is overusing his new word without fully grasping its meaning.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Gliding like a jabroni is what I'm gonna be doing.

jokes.text: What the hell is a podcast? Why don't you just tape it?
jokes.setup: Dennis and Dee talking about starting a podcast
jokes.punchline: Frank's old-fashioned confusion about digital technology
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frank's technological confusion, though somewhat predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: What the hell is a podcast? Why don't you just tape it?

jokes.text: So, Dennis and Dee Reynolds here. Inaugural podcast. We're talking technology today. The big 'T.'
jokes.setup: Dennis starts their first podcast attempt
jokes.punchline: His overly formal, pretentious introduction and calling technology 'the big T'
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis characterization - his pretentious attempt at professionalism.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: So, Dennis and Dee Reynolds here. Inaugural podcast. We're talking technology today. The big 'T.'

jokes.text: [Long pause after 'My opinion?']
jokes.setup: Dennis asking Dee for her opinion on technology
jokes.punchline: Dee has literally no thoughts and the dead air is uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Excellent awkward silence comedy. Dee's complete lack of thoughts creates perfect cringe moment.

jokes.text: Dead air! Could you ease up on the crackers?
jokes.setup: The podcast is already failing with dead air
jokes.punchline: Dennis adding to the chaos by complaining about Frank's crackers
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the podcast disaster. Multiple things going wrong simultaneously.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dead air! Could you ease up on the crackers?

jokes.text: It's from when Frank hit me with the trash can. Wound got infected. It was a whole ordeal.
jokes.setup: Cricket's voice sounds different
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Frank's violence and Cricket's resulting injury
jokes.explanation: Dark humor typical of Cricket's degradation. Casual mention of serious injury treated as minor inconvenience.

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: It's from when Frank hit me with the trash can. Wound got infected. It was a whole ordeal.

jokes.text: I lied about the chicken, Crick.
jokes.setup: Cricket demanding his promised rotisserie chicken
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual admission he never intended to provide it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank cruelty - manipulating Cricket then casually admitting the deception.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I lied about the chicken, Crick.

jokes.text: Lemons? You suck on 'em.
jokes.setup: Frank offering Cricket something instead of chicken
jokes.punchline: Giving him lemons and treating them like an adequate substitute
jokes.explanation: Absurd Frank logic. Lemons as food substitutes, explained as if this makes perfect sense.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Lemons? You suck on 'em.

jokes.text: I wake up, I find a dog sniffin' at my wound. He's fully aroused, mind you.
jokes.setup: Cricket describing a typical day as a homeless person
jokes.punchline: Disturbing detail about aroused dog showing sexual interest in his wound
jokes.explanation: Peak Sunny darkness. Cricket's degradation reaches disturbing new lows with matter-of-fact delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: I wake up, I find a dog sniffin' at my wound. He's fully aroused, mind you.

jokes.text: Does my scar look like a dog's vagina?
jokes.setup: Cricket wondering why the dog was interested
jokes.punchline: His genuinely philosophical question about anatomical similarity
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disturbing line that's both absurd and weirdly logical. Cricket's innocent philosophical approach to horrifying situation.

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: Does my scar look like a dog's vagina?

jokes.text: Not since that Chinaman stole my kidney.
jokes.setup: Cricket saying he doesn't believe in God anymore
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of organ theft using outdated terminology
jokes.explanation: More Cricket degradation mythology. Casual mention of major trauma with politically incorrect language.

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: Not since that Chinaman stole my kidney.

jokes.text: A dog banged your neck the other day!
jokes.setup: Argument between Dee and Cricket escalating
jokes.punchline: Dee's crude reference to Cricket's earlier dog story
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Cricket's dog story, escalating the argument with crude humor.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: A dog banged your neck the other day!

jokes.text: U.S.A! U.S.A! Not like that. Make it classy.
jokes.setup: Charlie coaching Mac on how to pump up the crowd
jokes.punchline: The idea that chanting USA can be done 'classily'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdity - the notion that there's a classy way to do a USA chant at a hockey game.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: U.S.A! U.S.A!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Not like that. Make it classy.

jokes.text: Next thing you know, I'm wearing a wig for the rest of my life just to keep up appearances. Yeah, like Andre Agassi, huh?
jokes.setup: Mac considering wearing a wig for dramatic effect
jokes.punchline: Reference to Andre Agassi's famous wig secret
jokes.explanation: Smart reference to real celebrity wig revelation. Shows Mac's awareness of how appearances can trap you.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Next thing you know, I'm wearing a wig for the rest of my life just to keep up appearances. Yeah, like Andre Agassi, huh?

jokes.text: All right, well, that would have been cool if I was standing backwards.
jokes.setup: Mac completely whiffs his practice shot
jokes.punchline: His attempt to find a positive spin on total failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac denial. Finding an impossible scenario where his failure would be success.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: All right, well, that would have been cool if I was standing backwards.

jokes.text: Only a couple of jabronis come out here without skates on.
jokes.setup: Rink employee kicks them out for not wearing skates
jokes.punchline: Mac calling themselves jabronis, still misusing the word
jokes.explanation: Good callback to jabroni usage. Mac continues to misapply his new favorite word.

dialogue.line: Only a couple of jabronis come out here without skates on.

jokes.text: I think it's some dago word, but it sounds like a hockey word.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking about 'jabroni'
jokes.punchline: Mac's casual ethnic slur while admitting he doesn't know what the word means
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's casual ignorance and prejudice while using words he doesn't understand.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I think it's some dago word, but it sounds like a hockey word.

jokes.text: That was a duck. Why would you make a duck noise right in the middle of my thing?
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to discuss stem cell research seriously
jokes.punchline: Dee making random duck noises to sabotage the podcast
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic - Dee undermining Dennis's pompous podcast attempts with juvenile behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That was a duck. Why would you make a duck noise right in the middle of my thing?

jokes.text: I thought I'd up the production value, add some sound effects.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking why she made duck noises
jokes.punchline: Dee's misguided belief that random animal sounds improve the show
jokes.explanation: Good Dee logic - completely missing what makes good radio while trying to help.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I thought I'd up the production value, add some sound effects.

jokes.text: How 'bout if we talk about how you used to bang each other?
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to interview a soldier about war
jokes.punchline: Frank derailing with inappropriate personal questions
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank inappropriate intervention. Sabotaging serious interview with sexual questions.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: How 'bout if we talk about how you used to bang each other?

jokes.text: Do you not know how to ice skate? No, not at all.
jokes.setup: Mac's been planning this hockey shot for the whole episode
jokes.punchline: Revelation that he can't even ice skate
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Mac's incompetence. The most basic requirement was never considered.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Do you not know how to ice skate?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, not at all.

jokes.text: But I figured you would teach me, 'cause we got plenty of time. We don't have time! It's tomorrow night, man!
jokes.setup: Mac revealing he can't skate
jokes.punchline: His assumption Charlie would teach him everything in one day
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Mac's poor planning and unrealistic expectations.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: But I figured you would teach me, 'cause we got plenty of time.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We don't have time! It's tomorrow night, man!

jokes.text: [Push it to the Limit montage while Mac learns to skate]
jokes.setup: Mac needs to learn skating in one day
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top 80s training montage for basic ice skating
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comedy. Epic training montage music for pathetically basic skill learning.

jokes.text: You're gonna pay me $500 to drink soup out of a shoe. And take your top off. Fine. I'll do it. But I am not taking my top off.
jokes.setup: Frank offering increasingly large sums for degrading acts
jokes.punchline: Waitress agreeing to drink from a shoe but drawing the line at nudity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the waitress's desperation and weird moral boundaries.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You're gonna pay me $500 to drink soup out of a shoe. And take your top off.

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: Fine. I'll do it. But I am not taking my top off.

jokes.text: Couldn't find soup, but I found some dirty dishwater.
jokes.setup: Cricket bringing soup for the waitress to drink
jokes.punchline: Making the degrading act even worse with contaminated water
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cricket contribution - making bad situation worse through incompetence and poor hygiene.

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: Couldn't find soup, but I found some dirty dishwater.

jokes.text: The future of radio, you jabronis.
jokes.setup: Radio hosts asking about the demo tape
jokes.punchline: Mac using 'jabronis' while promoting Dennis and Dee's terrible podcast
jokes.explanation: Final jabroni usage, now spreading to professional radio hosts showing Mac's influence.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: The future of radio, you jabronis.

jokes.text: Jabronis. Cool word.
jokes.setup: Mac calling the radio hosts jabronis
jokes.punchline: Professional radio host adopting Mac's misused word
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to jabroni running gag. Mac's ignorance spreads to actual media professionals.

dialogue.speaker: Radio Host
dialogue.line: Jabronis. Cool word.

jokes.text: No. You fell on your face almost immediately, man.
jokes.setup: Mac waking up thinking he succeeded
jokes.punchline: Charlie's blunt description of Mac's immediate failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Mac's fantasy. Brutal honesty about his spectacular failure.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: No. You fell on your face almost immediately, man.

jokes.text: Who took the shot? I took the shot. Yeah, I missed wide right by, like, a mile.
jokes.setup: Mac failed, so someone else had to take the shot
jokes.punchline: Charlie also failed spectacularly despite being Mac's coach
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - the 'expert' coach also completely failed. Both characters are equally incompetent.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Who took the shot?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I took the shot.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, I missed wide right by, like, a mile.

jokes.text: Look on the bright side, dude. I think you're gonna be a sports celebrity after all... 'cause they're definitely gonna play that shit on ESPN.
jokes.setup: Mac wanted to become a sports legend
jokes.punchline: Charlie pointing out he'll be famous for spectacular failure instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic ending. Mac gets his wish to be famous, just not in the way he wanted. Good character moment for Charlie's optimism.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Look on the bright side, dude. I think you're gonna be a sports celebrity after all... 'cause they're definitely gonna play that shit on ESPN.
